purpose: Read-only contract that compiles migration readiness planner findings into self-contained implementation checklists for agents and operators.

no_destructive_edit_policy.required_boundary: Recipes may tell an agent what to inspect and change manually, but they must not rewrite source files, create branches, create worktrees, delete files, or claim automated migration success.

fixture_projects.checklist_focus: prove the core crate remains tokio-free and keep compat crates out of the default runtime graph
route axum, hyper, tower, tonic, and request client findings into native web/grpc/service modules or explicit compat boundaries
keep hard Tokio dependencies inside asupersync-tokio-compat or another named edge adapter
separate default, metrics, fuzz, and workspace proof commands before changing dependency edges
thread Cx and explicit capabilities before any runtime replacement work
repair manifest readability before claiming migration readiness

concept_catalog.recipe_guidance: Replace orphan spawn sites with region-owned scope work and explicit Cx flow.
Use race/join patterns that cancel and drain losers before the region closes.
Map sends to reserve/send or cancel-aware primitives so obligations cannot leak on cancellation.
Route timers through runtime or lab-time surfaces and keep timeout losers cancel-drained.
Prefer native I/O and codec traits; use adapters only where a third-party edge requires them.
Preserve process cleanup, signal registration, and filesystem atomicity boundaries explicitly.
Use native HTTP where possible; otherwise keep hyper runtime traits behind the compat boundary.
Move owned gRPC surfaces to native modules and quarantine tonic transport behind adapters when necessary.
Convert route ownership, request state, and extractors to explicit Cx-aware web/service surfaces.
Keep middleware stacks at named native service or compat boundaries and record which layers remain bridge-only.
Prefer native HTTP client surfaces; quarantine reqwest-style clients behind the compat bridge when ownership is external.

proof_lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_migration_recipe_compiler_contract CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test migration_recipe_compiler_contract --no-default-features -- --nocapture
does_not_cover: migration correctness for an external project

proof_lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_tokio_default cargo tree -e normal -p asupersync -i tokio
RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_migration_readiness_planner cargo test -p asupersync --test migration_readiness_planner_contract -- --nocapture

pattern_changes: thread &Cx into entry points before replacing runtime calls
replace detached spawn sites with region-owned scope work
replace select losers with cancel-and-drain race or timeout patterns
replace channel send sites with reserve/send or explicit abort handling
compiled_recipe_examples.compat_boundary_policy: No compat boundary is accepted for owned application concurrency primitives.
generated_checklist: inventory spawn/select/channel/time findings from the planner report
assign each finding to an owning region and explicit Cx source
port one owned surface manually and add deterministic lab coverage for loser drain or no obligation leaks
run the listed proof lanes through RCH
record unresolved findings as owner beads instead of marking them migrated
no_claims: does not prove the external project is fully migrated
does not permit branch creation, worktree creation, deletion, or automated rewriting

pattern_changes: move owned handlers and request state to native web or service modules first
use hyper/tower/tonic compat adapters only for third-party edges that cannot be retired yet
write the compat boundary name and owner into the generated checklist
keep no-tokio core crate proof lanes separate from satellite compat proof lanes
compiled_recipe_examples.compat_boundary_policy: Compat is acceptable only at a named edge adapter with explicit Cx ownership and a residual-risk row.
generated_checklist: classify each web/grpc/client finding as owned-native, third-party-compat, or blocked-design-review
map owned-native findings to src/web, src/http, src/grpc, or src/service
map third-party-compat findings to asupersync-tokio-compat feature gates
run no-tokio production graph checks after each boundary change
document any remaining compat rows in the operator report
no_claims: does not claim parity with axum, hyper, tonic, tower, or reqwest APIs
does not make asupersync-tokio-compat part of the core runtime graph

pattern_changes: preserve codec frame boundaries and cancellation checkpoints
keep filesystem atomicity and symlink behavior in the platform matrix
make process cleanup and signal registration explicit in the checklist
route unsupported host surfaces to skip or blocked rows instead of green support
compiled_recipe_examples.compat_boundary_policy: Adapter use is deferred until a concrete third-party trait boundary is named.
generated_checklist: split I/O trait findings from fs/process/signal findings
attach each finding to its cookbook or playbook evidence path
port the smallest owned surface manually
add deterministic tests for cancellation, cleanup, or host-specific skip behavior
leave unsupported host behavior as an explicit no-claim row
no_claims: does not prove cross-platform filesystem or signal parity
does not replace the platform capability matrix

validation.rch_command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_migration_recipe_compiler_contract CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test migration_recipe_compiler_contract --no-default-features -- --nocapture

no_claims: This contract does not compile or modify an external project.
This contract does not prove broad workspace health, release readiness, runtime correctness, performance, or live RCH fleet availability.
This contract does not authorize deleting files, creating branches, creating worktrees, or running automated codemods.
This contract does not claim that any particular third-party migration is complete without project-specific planner output and proof lanes.
